programmes.show_description: The Person in Your Ear: Thrilling spy drama. Weir is in 'go mode' to unveil the truth behind Valence's death as he enlists Hailey's help to exploit a member of Crowley's network.
Just Bring the Darn Fish!: US cooking competition. The infamous surf and turf relay challenge splits the chefs as they taste and identify mystery proteins as quickly as possible.
The Long Ride Back to Scratchwood: Comedy-drama series. Arthur scores an own goal when he is persuaded to buy 2,000 tickets for an England v Scotland football match at Wembley.
Jack or Knave?: Crime drama. Regan behaves with his usual disregard for authority, but he finds himself in deep trouble when an old allegation of corruption rears its ugly head.
Shopping from home.
The latest news and reviews from the world of cars.
Shopping from home.
A short sports film about the legendary racehorse.
Brian Moore presents action from the 1984/85 football season including Newcastle United vs Manchester United, Cambridge United vs York City, and Blackburn Rovers vs Fulham.
Brian Moore presents FA Cup Fifth Round action from the 1984/85 season - highlights include Third Division York City against Liverpool and Everton facing non-league Telford United.
A Little Bit of Luck... A Little Bit of Grief: Classic US action drama series. Rick wins one million dollars in a lottery while visiting mainland America and gains a new fiancee.
The Condemned: Classic US detective drama. Theo tries to help a desperate boxer falsely accused of murdering his wife when he takes a priest and two of his parishioners hostage.
Lost on the Range: Classic drama. A prize stallion goes missing while Ken and his faithful posse are called upon to provide security for some local stables.
Jack or Knave?: The final episode of the classic '70s police drama series. Regan finds himself in deep trouble after an old allegation of corruption rears its ugly head.
In with the New: US robot combat series. Top bots including Tantrum, SawBlaze and Madcatter put it all on the line with World Championship qualification on their minds.
Oli Bell presents live coverage of racing from Newcastle and Lingfield. Today's highlights are at Newcastle, where the all-weather season comes to a close.
From Nijinsky to Tiger Roll, Ed Chamberlin introduces some of the great racehorses of the modern era - enjoy Best Mate's third Gold Cup and Shergar racing to Derby glory.
Asian Slayer: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the Himalayas to investigate a fresh spate of savage river attacks and learns that the suspected culprit is a sacred animal.
The Balance of Power: Comedy-drama series. Arthur decides to stand as a candidate in a local council by-election when his used car lot is threatened by a compulsory purchase order.
The Grave Vault: Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald visit a self-described one-legged hay farmer whose pieces include a cast-iron coffin.
Comedy crime caper film starring Burt Reynolds and Sally Field, 1977. A legendary truck driver is hired to transport an illegal beer shipment from Texas to Georgia within 28 hours.
Latest news from the world of entertainment.
Comedy crime caper film starring Burt Reynolds and Sally Field, 1977. A legendary truck driver is hired to transport an illegal beer shipment from Texas to Georgia within 28 hours.
Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling - the roster features world-class talent including Jon Moxley, MJF, Ricochet and Adam Page.
Action from the Good Friday fixtures in the Football League, including Coventry v Derby, Middlesbrough v Millwall, Leicester v Preston and West Brom v Wrexham in the Championship.
Second Hand Pose: Drama series. After being left in an industrial freezer, Terry looks for alternative employment and gets work helping out in the second-hand furniture business.
Camel Filters and Pheromones: American sitcom about a bachelor whose life is disrupted when his brother and young nephew move in. The boys are distracted by Berta's granddaughter.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
This programme brings you up to date with the latest action from the world of motorsport and features race coverage, insights, and behind-the-scenes footage from the best series.
Shopping from home.
From Nijinsky to Tiger Roll, Ed Chamberlin introduces some of the great racehorses of the modern era - enjoy Best Mate's third Gold Cup and Shergar racing to Derby glory.
Brother Hood: Adventure series. A reclusive industrial magnate offers an assignment to Harry that involves the engineering of his brother's escape from a Mediterranean island prison.
Brian Moore presents action from the 1984/85 football season including highlights of Arsenal vs Aston Villa, Grimsby Town vs Wolves, and Bristol City vs Bristol Rovers.
Action from the Good Friday fixtures in the Football League, including Coventry v Derby, Middlesbrough v Millwall, Leicester v Preston and West Brom v Wrexham in the Championship.
Magazine show to complement coverage of ITV horse racing - it is presented on-site from the main course each Saturday with additional weekday programmes during the big festivals.
Brian Moore introduces football highlights from November 1971, including a full-blooded London derby between Chelsea and Tottenham, and action from Nottingham Forest v Leeds.
Kings of the Canvas: Richard Henwood takes a look back at the glory days of British wrestling with some classic action from the archives.
Meet the family firm making traditional haggis, learn what goes into making a set of bowls, discover how wheelbarrows are made and meet a team who make classic brogue shoes.
The Even Chance: The first episode of the drama series. 1793 - 17-year-old Horatio Hornblower begins his career in His Majesty's Navy and must prove himself when a war is declared.
The Examination for Lieutenant: Drama series about CS Forester's famous seafaring hero. Promoted to acting lieutenant, Hornblower is forced to question his readiness for command.
Petrol Heads: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene, and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt.
Live ice hockey action from the NHL as the New York Rangers host the Detroit Red Wings at Madison Square Garden in an Eastern Division matchup.
American Killers: Jeremy Wade travels to one of the largest dams in the Americas on the trail of a river monster that is responsible for the mysterious disappearance of corpses.
Last Orders: Richard Henwood takes a look back at The Indoor League, a TV show featuring 1970s pub games and presented by Yorkshire cricketing great Fred Trueman.
A selection of nine-dart finishes from ITV Sport's darts coverage - every darts player strives to produce one, and this prowess is even more impressive in front of the TV cameras.
A selection of goals from some of the superstars of the game - including Kevin Keegan and Paul Gascoigne - who played in the old First Division in the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s.
Bond film starring Roger Moore and Carole Bouquet, 1981. 007 searches for a vital communication device that gives its bearer unlimited control over Britain's nuclear submarines.
